steps.title: Identify the clause types and the punctuation rule
steps.content:
The sentence reads: Antonio Martorell's 2010 woodcut print La Piaya Negra I (Tar Beach I) has been displayed at the Smithsonian American Art ______ features a large collection of contemporary art. The main clause ends at Museum; which features a large collection of contemporary art is a non-restrictive relative clause that adds extra information. A non-restrictive relative clause introduced by which requires a comma before it.
  • Choice A — ‘Museum and which’: "And which" is non-standard; you cannot coordinate a relative clause with "and which" unless the sentence already has a parallel "which" clause. ✗
  • Choice B — ‘Museum; which’: A semicolon joins two independent clauses, but a relative clause beginning with "which" is not independent. ✗
  • Choice C — ‘Museum. Which’: Starting a new sentence with a relative pronoun creates a fragment because "which" has no antecedent clause to attach to. ✗
  • Choice D — ‘Museum, which’: A comma before the non-restrictive relative clause which features a large collection of contemporary art follows the standard rule for adding supplementary information. ✓

steps.title: Confirm by applying the rule
steps.content: Non-restrictive relative clauses introduced by which are set off from the main clause by a comma. Choice D (‘Museum, which’) is the only option that uses this structure correctly. That confirms D is correct.
